title: Improve login error reporting with non-TTY stdin
body:
I am trying to do skopeo login and then skopeo sync in gitlab ci with a docker runner (ideally a rootless k8s runner, but trying on docker atm).

I am getting this error. What does it mean? 

How do I use "the Docker-compatible file path option", and what does "inappropriate ioctl for device" mean? 

```
$ skopeo login  -u "$REGISTRY_USERNAME" -p "$REGISTRY_PASSWORD" "$REGISTRY_HOST"
time="2025-07-08T16:09:18Z" level=warning msg="$REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE points to ~/.docker/config.json, but the file format is not fully compatible; use the Docker-compatible file path option instead"
No credentials found for my_registry.azurecr.io
Password: time="2025-07-08T16:09:18Z" level=fatal msg="getting username and password: reading password: inappropriate ioctl for device"
```

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

“The Docker-compatible file path option” is, for `login`, `--compat-auth-file`. But it is not exposed for `skopeo sync` currently.

“inappropriate ioctl for device” almost certainly means that the code is trying to _prompt_ for a password; that should happen only if the parameter to `-p` is empty. Maybe some scripting mistake?

---

At a higher level: inside an automation, it should not be necessary to do a `skopeo login` at all.
- Run `skopeo login` with `--authfile`, in some manual setup step, perhaps several times: Collect all relevant credentials inside a single auth file.
- Store the _whole auth file_ as a secret / input to the automation.
- Then, in the automated process, run only `skopeo sync --authfile=$my_authfile_from_secret`. That way, the operation is isolated from the larger runtime state (neither reading nor writing credentials used for some other purpose), gets _exactly_ a controlled set of credentials; and it avoids registry round-trips to verify the password during `login`.
@mtrmac - Thank you. I'll try that authfile approach tomorrow, when I am back in the office!

> “inappropriate ioctl for device” almost certainly means that the code is trying to prompt for a password; that should happen only if the parameter to -p is empty. Maybe some scripting mistake?

BTW if this is the case, please say so — we should improve the error handling.

@mtrmac - The password was empty. You were spot on. The variable was only exposed to main branch, but not to a Merge Request.... Spent way too long debugging that. 

Finally got something that works. 

<details>

<summary>Working GitLab CI config</summary>

```yaml
login:
  image: ubuntu
  tags: [k8s-small]
  needs: []
  stage: sync
  variables:
    GIT_STRATEGY: none
  script:
    - |
      # Fail if any required variable is unset or empty
      for var in FOO_REGISTRY_USERNAME FOO_REGISTRY_PASSWORD HARBOR_REGISTRY_USER HARBOR_REGISTRY_PASSWORD; do
        if [ -z "${!var}" ]; then
          echo "Error: Environment variable $var is not set or is empty"
          exit 1
        fi
      done
    - export FOO_AUTH=$(printf "%s:%s" "$FOO_REGISTRY_USERNAME" "$FOO_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" | base64 -w 0)
    - export HARBOR_AUTH=$(printf "%s:%s" "$HARBOR_REGISTRY_USER" "$HARBOR_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" | base64 -w 0)
    - >
      jq -n --arg HARBOR_AUTH "$HARBOR_AUTH" --arg FOO_AUTH "$FOO_AUTH" '{
        auths: {
          "harbor.company.com": { auth: $HARBOR_AUTH },
          "foo.azurecr.io": { auth: $FOO_AUTH }
        }
      }' > auth.json
  artifacts:
    when: on_success
    access: all
    expire_in: 1 hour
    paths:
      - auth.json

copy images:
  image:
    name: quay.io/skopeo/stable
    entrypoint: [""]
  tags: [docker]
  needs: [login]
  stage: sync
  variables:
    GIT_STRATEGY: none
  script:
    - |
      if [ ! -s auth.json ]; then
        echo "auth.json does not exist or is empty"
        exit 1
      fi
    - skopeo copy --authfile auth.json docker://${FROM} docker://${TO}
  parallel:
    matrix:
      - FROM: "$FOO_REGISTRY_HOST/example_one:$DATAAPI_TAG"
        TO: "$HARBOR_REGISTRY_PREFIX/example_one:$CI_COMMIT_SHA"
      - FROM: "$FOO_REGISTRY_HOST/example_two:$DATAAPI_TAG"
        TO: "$HARBOR_REGISTRY_PREFIX/example_two:$CI_COMMIT_SHA"
      - FROM: "$FOO_REGISTRY_HOST/example_three:$BILLING_TAG"
        TO: "$HARBOR_REGISTRY_PREFIX/example_three:$BILLING_TAG"
... add more images to copy
```

</details>
